refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges both on-site and remote operation modes (Teleoperation stakeholder). The refined FR now clearly states the performance boundary (productivity_target) and the evaluation context (representative operations, agreed period).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 40 operating hours). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) via onboard instrumentation and/or site survey. Compare against productivity_target. Document compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies via safety audit and operator logs.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time and payload capacity sufficient to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor Vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall enforce operational constraints (speed limits, geofence, emergency stop) such that safety compliance does not degrade productivity below productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time Telemetry (cycle time, payload, position, fuel consumption) to enable productivity tracking and operator feedback.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-capacity hydraulic excavator with optimized bucket geometry, fast cycle hydraulics, and payload monitoring; pair with remote operation capability and safety interlocks to achieve target productivity.
DP-B: Deploy autonomous or semi-autonomous earthmoving system with AI-driven task planning, real-time obstacle avoidance, and predictive maintenance to maximize utilization and minimize idle time.

DP-C: Implement operator-in-the-loop teleoperated vehicle with low-latency network link, high-fidelity sensor suite (LIDAR, stereo camera, IMU), and safety-critical control layer to enable remote productivity equivalent to on-site operation.

DP-D: Hybrid approach: Vehicle with baseline autonomous task execution (dig, load, dump cycles) augmented by Teleoperation for complex scenarios and Safety Services for constraint enforcement; Telemetry feedback loop for continuous productivity optimization.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid approach—Vehicle with baseline autonomous task execution (dig, load, dump cycles) augmented by Teleoperation for complex scenarios and Safety Services for constraint enforcement; Telemetry feedback loop for continuous productivity optimization.

potential_sources: Coupling between Vehicle autonomous logic and Teleoperation command authority (must define clear handoff and priority rules).

Coupling between Safety Services constraint enforcement and productivity optimization (safety limits may reduce cycle speed; must validate that safety-constrained operation still meets productivity_target).
Coupling between Telemetry data collection and real-time control loop (latency in telemetry feedback could degrade operator situational awareness in remote mode).
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is a composite design that distributes responsibility across Vehicle (autonomous baseline), Teleoperation (remote override), and Safety Services (constraint layer). Couplings are manageable via clear interface contracts (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry) and priority arbitration logic. Hybrid approach reduces single-point-of-failure risk and allows graceful degradation (e.g., if network fails, Vehicle continues autonomous operation under safety constraints).

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Autonomous task execution (dig, load, dump) is technically feasible within project timeline and cost envelope.
ASSUMPTION: Network latency for Teleoperation is ≤ 200 ms round-trip, sufficient for operator control without safety compromise.
ASSUMPTION: Safety Services can enforce constraints (geofence, speed limit, emergency stop) without blocking all productive motion.
RISK: If autonomous logic fails or network link drops, Vehicle must safely halt or revert to safe idle state; fallback behavior must be validated.
RISK: Productivity_target may be unachievable if safety constraints are too restrictive (e.g., very low speed limits in congested worksite); target may need re-negotiation.
RISK: Operator training burden increases with hybrid mode (must understand when to intervene, how to override autonomous logic safely).
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the decision logic for mode switching (autonomous vs. teleoperated) and the priority arbitration when both systems issue commands. Define explicit safety interlocks (e.g., Teleoperation cannot override emergency stop, Safety Services can veto any command that violates geofence). Validate that productivity_target is achievable under worst-case safety constraints via simulation or prototype trial.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration boundary. Encompasses Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and their interconnections. Responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery, safety compliance, and operator experience (on-site and remote). Owns the Telemetry aggregation, Tasking arbitration, and SafetyAlert routing.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time productivity metrics (cycle time, payload, position, fuel consumption, equipment health) streamed to Site Manager dashboard and Tele-operator console. Enables productivity tracking and operator feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: High-level task commands from Site Manager or Tele-operator (e.g., 'excavate zone A', 'load truck B', 'move to staging area'). NGHE Solution decomposes into Vehicle autonomous subtasks or Teleoperation directives.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety-critical alerts (geofence breach, emergency stop triggered, operator override, equipment fault) routed to Safety Manager and Site Manager. Enables real-time safety monitoring and incident response.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel (cellular, satellite, or private network) connecting Vehicle, Teleoperation, and remote operator console. Carries Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and low-level control commands.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for this FR. It owns the end-to-end productivity delivery logic, integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, and manages all external interfaces. Minimal diffusion: only Telemetry and SafetyAlert leak to external stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager), which is intentional and necessary for visibility.

Vehicle is responsible for physical task execution (dig, load, dump) and contributes significantly to productivity. However, Vehicle alone cannot achieve the FR: it lacks Teleoperation capability for remote operation, lacks Safety Services for constraint enforcement, and lacks Telemetry aggregation for productivity tracking. Assigning FR to Vehicle would diffuse responsibility for remote operation and safety compliance to external modules.

Teleoperation enables remote operator control and contributes to productivity in complex scenarios. However, Teleoperation alone cannot achieve the FR: it depends on Vehicle for physical execution, depends on Safety Services for constraint enforcement, and does not own Telemetry aggregation. Assigning FR to Teleoperation would diffuse responsibility for autonomous task execution and safety compliance.

Safety Services enforces operational constraints (geofence, speed limit, emergency stop) and is critical for safe productivity. However, Safety Services alone cannot achieve the FR: it does not own Vehicle execution, does not own Teleoperation, and does not own Telemetry. Assigning FR to Safety Services would diffuse responsibility for task execution and remote operation.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because it owns the end-to-end productivity delivery logic, integrates all sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services), and manages all external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link). The FR is fundamentally a system-level acceptance criterion that cannot be satisfied by any single sub-system in isolation.
containment_risks: RISK: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration boundary; detailed ownership of productivity optimization logic (e.g., cycle time tuning, payload balancing) may be unclear. Recommend explicit allocation of sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and a Telemetry/Monitoring module.

RISK: If NGHE Solution is treated as a 'virtual' integration layer without dedicated engineering team, accountability for productivity delivery may be diffuse. Recommend assigning a System Integration team or Product Owner to NGHE Solution.

RISK: Telemetry aggregation and Tasking arbitration logic must be implemented somewhere (likely in a central controller or cloud service). Ensure this logic is explicitly owned and not left as implicit responsibility.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that NGHE Solution has a dedicated System Integration team or Product Owner responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery, mode switching logic, and interface management. Allocate sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and a Telemetry/Monitoring module with clear ownership boundaries. Define explicit interface contracts (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) with latency, bandwidth, and reliability requirements.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (hybrid autonomous + teleoperated + safety-constrained approach) directly addresses the refined FR (achieve productivity_target while maintaining safety compliance). DP is logically sound, feasible, and aligns with stakeholder needs (Operator, Tele-operator, Safety Manager). No uncoupled or missing functional requirements identified.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry aggregation logic (cycle time, payload, position tracking) must be owned by a specific module (recommend Telemetry/Monitoring sub-module within NGHE Solution or Vehicle). If left unowned, diffusion risk is high.

Tasking arbitration logic (decomposing high-level tasks into Vehicle autonomous subtasks or Teleoperation directives) must be owned by NGHE Solution or a dedicated Task Planner module. If left unowned, diffusion risk is high.

Mode switching logic (autonomous vs. teleoperated) and priority arbitration (when both systems issue commands) must be explicitly owned and tested. If left implicit, safety and productivity risks are high.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for this FR. All sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution's scope. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are clearly defined and owned by NGHE Solution. Recommend explicit allocation of sub-FRs to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry/Monitoring to reduce diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trial (40+ operating hours) with productivity measurement (m³/h or ton/h) and safety audit. Telemetry system must provide accurate cycle time, payload, and position data. Acceptance criteria (productivity_target, safety compliance) must be quantified and agreed with customer before trial.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry/Monitoring respectively. Each sub-FR is atomic and verifiable. Recommend explicit traceability matrix linking FR-NGHE-001 → sub-FRs → DPs → IMs.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit interface contracts for Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link with latency, bandwidth, reliability, and data format specifications.
Allocate sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry/Monitoring with clear ownership and verification criteria.
Define mode switching logic (autonomous vs. teleoperated) and priority arbitration rules. Validate via simulation and prototype trial.
Quantify productivity_target and safety constraints (geofence, speed limit, emergency stop response time) with customer. Validate that target is achievable under worst-case constraints.
Establish Telemetry system requirements (accuracy, latency, availability) to support real-time productivity tracking and operator feedback.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that has been successfully refined into four atomic sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4), each addressing a distinct aspect of productivity delivery (Vehicle execution, Teleoperation capability, Safety Services constraints, Telemetry feedback). The selected DP (hybrid autonomous + teleoperated + safety-constrained) is logically sound and feasible. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is the correct top-level boundary. However, the sub-FRs are not yet fully decomposed into detailed design parameters and implementation modules. Recommend proceeding to the next decomposition level to allocate sub-FRs to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry/Monitoring modules and define their detailed DPs and IMs.
decision.justification_detail: This is a system-level FR that requires hierarchical decomposition. The current level establishes the top-level architecture (hybrid approach, NGHE Solution as integrator). The next level must detail how each sub-system (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, Telemetry) contributes to productivity and how they interact via defined interfaces. Proceeding to ZIGZAG_DOWN allows systematic allocation of responsibility and verification of each sub-system's contribution to the overall productivity target.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time and payload capacity sufficient to achieve productivity_target. [Allocated to Vehicle IM]

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor Vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain productivity_target. [Allocated to Teleoperation IM]

FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall enforce operational constraints (speed limits, geofence, emergency stop) such that safety compliance does not degrade productivity below productivity_target. [Allocated to Safety Services IM]

FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time Telemetry (cycle time, payload, position, fuel consumption) to enable productivity tracking and operator feedback. [Allocated to Telemetry/Monitoring sub-module within NGHE Solution]

assumptions: Autonomous task execution (dig, load, dump) is technically feasible within project timeline and cost envelope.
Network latency for Teleoperation is ≤ 200 ms round-trip, sufficient for operator control without safety compromise.
Safety Services can enforce constraints (geofence, speed limit, emergency stop) without blocking all productive motion.
Productivity_target is achievable under worst-case safety constraints (to be validated via simulation or prototype trial).
Telemetry system can provide accurate cycle time, payload, and position data with latency ≤ 1 second for real-time operator feedback.
Site Manager and Tele-operator will provide high-level Tasking commands; NGHE Solution will decompose into detailed Vehicle and Teleoperation directives.
open_risks: RISK: If autonomous logic fails or network link drops, Vehicle must safely halt or revert to safe idle state; fallback behavior must be validated.
RISK: Productivity_target may be unachievable if safety constraints are too restrictive; target may need re-negotiation.
RISK: Operator training burden increases with hybrid mode; training program must be developed and validated.
RISK: Mode switching logic and priority arbitration between autonomous and teleoperated commands must be carefully designed and tested to avoid safety hazards.
RISK: Telemetry system latency or inaccuracy could degrade operator situational awareness in remote mode; must validate telemetry performance under representative conditions.
RISK: Network link reliability is critical for remote operation; must define fallback behavior and acceptable latency/packet loss thresholds.
evidence_needed: Simulation or prototype trial demonstrating that hybrid autonomous + teleoperated approach achieves productivity_target under representative worksite conditions.
Safety analysis (FMEA, FTA) validating that mode switching logic and priority arbitration do not introduce new safety hazards.
Network performance analysis (latency, bandwidth, reliability) confirming that Teleoperation can be executed with acceptable operator control fidelity.
Telemetry system validation confirming that cycle time, payload, and position data are accurate and timely enough for real-time operator feedback.
Customer agreement on productivity_target, safety constraints (geofence, speed limit, emergency stop response time), and evaluation criteria (representative operations, agreed period).
Operator training program and validation confirming that operators can safely and effectively use hybrid mode.
